prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a tight crowd of expressive little characters walking in step across a plaza, each one tethered to several neighbors by gentle elastic threads so that when one leans the others sway too, while high above a few faint translucent giant hands tug subtly on the whole group; one character glances around realizing it can no longer move without the rest. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A crowd of cartoon characters walking in lockstep, each tethered to neighbors by elastic threads and tugged from above by faint giant hands, one realizing it cannot move alone, dramatizing series that move together under shared dynamics and hidden factors.
caption: When series stop being independent columns and start leaning on one another, the only honest move is to model the whole crowd at once.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: four or five expressive characters standing in a ring, each gripping taut strings that run to every other character at once, so the whole web is interconnected; when one character takes a step the strings yank all the others slightly off balance, and every face wears the same mix of effort and resignation at being pulled in all directions. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Five cartoon characters in a ring, each holding strings connected to every other character, all tugged off balance whenever one moves, illustrating a vector autoregression where every series is regressed on the recent past of every series at once.
caption: In a VAR everything predicts everything: each series is regressed on the recent past of all the others, so one nudge ripples through the entire system.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cartoon detective in a coat crouches with a magnifying glass beside a proud crowing rooster on a fence, carefully jotting in a notepad that the crow reliably comes just before the sunrise glowing on the horizon, yet the detective holds up a cautioning hand toward the sun as if refusing to credit the rooster with causing the dawn; the rooster looks smug, the detective looks scrupulously skeptical. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cartoon detective with a magnifying glass notes that a crowing rooster reliably precedes the sunrise, while raising a cautioning hand to refuse crediting the rooster with causing the dawn, illustrating that Granger causality is prediction, not real intervention.
caption: Granger causality only says the rooster's crow helps forecast the dawn; it never claims the rooster makes the sun rise.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: two tipsy cartoon wanderers stagger along a winding path in wildly random directions, yet a short rope tied between their waists keeps snapping taut and reeling them back together whenever the gap grows, so however far each individually wobbles the distance between them always springs back to a bounded span; both look amiably oblivious, the rope doing the quiet work. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Two tipsy cartoon wanderers stagger randomly along a path but a short rope between their waists keeps snapping taut and pulling them back whenever the gap widens, illustrating cointegration where two random walks share a stable bounded long-run equilibrium.
caption: Each random walk wanders without limit, yet the rope of cointegration keeps the gap between them bounded and reliably mean-reverting.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: high on a hidden gantry two or three small puppeteers calmly work a few control bars, and from those bars a vast fan of strings descends to a huge crowd of a hundred dangling marionettes far below that all sway and dance in eerie unison; an observer at the bottom stares up baffled at the synchronized crowd, unaware of the handful of hands actually moving them. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Two or three hidden puppeteers on a high gantry work a few control bars whose strings move a hundred marionettes swaying in unison below, while a baffled observer looks up, illustrating dynamic factor models where a few latent factors drive hundreds of co-moving series.
caption: A hundred series can dance in unison while only a handful of hidden factors are actually pulling the strings.

after_heading: Volatility Modeling: ARCH, GARCH, and Stochastic Volatility
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a long stretch of sea where the water sits glassy and calm for a wide span, then suddenly erupts into a dense bunched run of towering choppy waves and dark swirling storm clouds packed close together, then settles flat and calm again, then bunches into another tight cluster of big waves; a small expressive boat rides serene through the calm and grips on for dear life through each clustered burst, showing turbulence arriving in bunches rather than evenly spread. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A sea that is glassy calm for long stretches then erupts into dense bunched runs of towering waves and storm clouds before settling again, with a small boat serene through calm and clinging through each clustered burst, illustrating volatility clustering where turbulent periods arrive in bunches.
caption: Volatility arrives in clusters: calm follows calm and turbulence follows turbulence, which is exactly what a GARCH model is built to capture.
